nodes.title: Positive
widgets_values: a girl sitting in her bedroom talking to the viewer on a beach, messy hair, strait teeth, wink

nodes.title: Open Pose
widgets_values:
These two make an openpose video to copy.

You can use either of them or another if you have another way for pose.  dont include the face here.

This allows you to copy simple movements, hands, and body to copy the video.

nodes.title: Info and window size
widgets_values:
Face strength
-Making this too strong copies the video face too much

0.6-0.9 worked well depending how complex the movement and change are.

Pose strength can be what you need, but 1 worked decent.

I did not change other settings in sampler.  You can play with them if you want.

Prompt backgrounds, subject details, etc.

I used 8 steps.. no idea if it can do less, but it seemed like it could perhaps do 4

eular is default, no idea if there is one better.

CFG 1 with lightx2v

shift 0-8 (5 default)

Seed fixed but you do you.

Models are fp8 scaled (v2 works best with wrapper)
https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy_fp8_scaled/tree/main/Wan22Animate

I use relight lora and wan lightx2v, both text to video and image to video lightx2v work.
And yes im using the wan 2.1 versions of lightx2v.  Either t2v or i2v lightx2v work here.
higher rank tends to work better than lower but take longer.
Found
https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy/tree/main
https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy/tree/main/Lightx2v
https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy/tree/main/LoRAs/Wan22_relight

Block swap (i have 12gb vram)
200 frames at 640x480 more frames doesnt tend to make vram jump too much, size does.
20 - 98% of vram, slow
30 - 82% of vram, faster
40 - 50% of vram, decent

if doing 480p i just block swap 40 because if not it can vram error part way for seemingly no reason.
at 480p (848x480) 40 block swap uses 57% of my 12gb vram.  But 35 uses 95%.  I just play it safe and swap all 40.  Slower but does not waste time doing 80% then erroring.

If using 8gb 40 probably works best.
If running more than 12gb, go as low as you can without maxing your vram to 95%+

Clip - either of these work
https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Wan_2.1_ComfyUI_repackaged/tree/main/split_files/text_encoders
or for NSFW
https://huggingface.co/NSFW-API/NSFW-Wan-UMT5-XXL/tree/main

This uses native clip loader and prompts because it uses much less vram to load.  It does not have the advanced prompting found in the wrapper clip loader like staggered prompting.

VAE - FP32 works best for merges, either work
https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy/tree/main

nodes.title: Face VIdeo
widgets_values:
This crops your videos face and creates a cropped video of the face to use to copy expressions, or lipsync.

This uses simple sam 1 face models to quickly detect the face in the video.
These masks do not need to be accurate, just be able to find the face to crop it out.

bbox thresh - change this lower to detect more, higher to detect less

drop size - this drops any masks smaller than this number usefull to remove background faces.

Post dilation - default -40.  This sets the crop closer, can adjust this as needed.

Padding
-this allows further adjusting of the cutout area, while making it square.


Once detections are done, it will show you the masks and a preview of the face video sent to ensure its cropped properly.

The size of the cutout sent can be set with the Size (square) node
- default 512x512, can do what you wish here

The bbox models are in the model manager for comfyui.

OPTIONS

Combine all masks
- enable this to combine all masks into 1 for the crop.  This fixes any missing frames as it crops the same area from all frames.  But this does crop further out and can cause the problems with the copy if cropped too far.  

Main reason to use this is when the person turns around quickly and it cant crop those frames.  This will fix that issue.  It can also help when the face video output is jumpy due to masking issues.

nodes.title: Main video loading
widgets_values:
This loads the video you want to copy and change.

Set fps here.  It can do 15-60fps, higher is better.  25-30 seems best.

This will rescale the video render to the size you set for the size of the longest side.

TURN OFF sampling group (below) until you have everything set up and ready, and have run the flow to load, crop, and preprocess everything.  then enable the sampling.

nodes.title: Nodes
widgets_values:
wan video wrapper
https://github.com/kijai/ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper

Essentials
https://github.com/cubiq/ComfyUI_essentials

Impact pack
https://github.com/ltdrdata/ComfyUI-Impact-Pack
https://github.com/ltdrdata/ComfyUI-Impact-Subpack

rgthree
https://github.com/rgthree/rgthree-comfy

kjnodes
https://github.com/kijai/ComfyUI-KJNodes

Video helper suite
https://github.com/Kosinkadink/ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite

Reactor
https://github.com/Gourieff/ComfyUI-ReActor

masquerade nodes
https://github.com/BadCafeCode/masquerade-nodes-comfyui

Simple ref image, it is rescaled to 2x your video size to ensure scaling for video is proper.

It runs a face restore from reactor, you can remove that node if you want.  I find it helps animate the face.

I recommend cropping this image to match as close as you can to the crop of the first frame (or a frame that shows everything) My cropping methood inside comfy is currently bugged so you need to do this yourself.

Just matching full body, half body, or just face helps. Closer the crop is to the first frame the better it copies.

Sets clip vision and the ref image
The image background will be used to generate.

While this does not need to match the first frame, it helps.

Clip vision h in manager
Both the wan 2.1 or the ip adapter seem to work.

https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Wan_2.1_ComfyUI_repackaged/tree/main/split_files/clip_vision

nodes.title: After Options
widgets_values:
WARNING
DONT RUN ANY OF THESE UNTIL YOU SAVE YOUR VIDEO TO THE LEFT

Test face restore models
- Allows you to try single frame with the face restore models to find one that does the best.

Unbatch - Option to force comfy to process each frame 1 by 1.  Slower but less memory problems.

Model upscale - Option to use a model to upscale further

face restore - Option to use reactor face restore to fix minor face issues with the sync.

frame interpolation - can be used if you dropped the fps to 15 or such.

nodes.title: Workflow info
widgets_values:
Was not going to put this out because i did not think it worked well enough but others may find it useful.
I only use this for meme lip syncs, anything else i use vace 2.1.

Wan 2.2 animate Wrapper version

This uses wan animate and wan video wrapper to make long form lip synced videos.  It does well for what it does.  But it has limits and some issues.

Issues

1. Wan wrapper does not let me load GGUF, so scaled fp8 are used.  
2. This is not set up to inpaint.  I could have sworn inpainting worked but i cant connect a mask set to the mask input without error.
  - trying to make a native comfy flow that does inpaint later.  Either im going crazy or the wrapper cant inpaint?
3. This cant do complex stuff.  This is an talking avitar copier.  Dont try to do anything difficult.
4. The smaller you make the video, the worse the ref image copy will be. 480p seems smallest you can go without loosing the subject. Unless its a close up face shot.


Enable the options you want, you dont need pose, face, or a ref image.  Just 1 works or any combo of them depending how much of the video you want to copy.

If you use pose it will make a video following the movement
If you use use face video it will make a video copying the face movement
If you use a ref image it will copy the image subject and background and make a video from it.

I actually find it works best with just a face video and ref image used as a start frame with no pose info.  Prompting what you want the movement to be.

nodes.title: Info and window size
widgets_values:
Window size is the only option here,

You can set desired batches and it should tell you how many frames your window needs to be to stagger the video amoung the windows.  

Choose a frame count 1 set higher than it says.

if it says you need 69, use 73,
if it says you need 83, use 85,

Follow wan 2.1 and 2.2 size and length conventions for the windows 
Frame counts of multiples of 4+1  (65, 81, 101, 125, etc)

Desired batches does not change a setting, you need to set the windows size here to your choice.

This will tell you frame count, duration, size and fps being used.
